title: Lasallea walteri
intro: Lasallea walteri, also known as the Walter's lasallea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Adoxaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasallea walteri include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasallea walteri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Lasallea walteri is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It was previously classified as a member of the genus Rhododendron but was reclassified in 2005 as part of the genus Lasallea. This plant is native to the southeastern United States, and it thrives in environments with acidic soils and partial shade. The classification of Lasallea walteri reflects ongoing research in plant taxonomy and systematics. The reclassification of this species has provided a more accurate understanding of its place within the plant kingdom.
